title: Abutments Act
text:
ABUTMENTS [CH.270 – 1
ABUTMENTS
CHAPTER 270
ABUTMENTS
ARRANGEMENT OF SECTIONS
SECTION
1. Short title.
2. Interpretation.
3. Empowers proprietors of certain lots of land to build out abutments.
4. Director of Public Works to grant certificates of abutments being
properly built — right of ownership vested.
5. Right of ownership conferred on persons who have already built out
abutments.
6. Restriction on buildings.
7. Repair of abutments.
8. Minister to cause public abutments to be built out, etc., and to have
public slips filled in-power to defray all expenses.
9. Abutments, slips and other public grounds placed under supervision of
the minister.
10. Minister empowered to sanction the erection of staircases.
–––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––
[Original Service 2001] STATUTE LAW OF THE BAHAMAS

ABUTMENTS [CH.270 – 3
CHAPTER 270
ABUTMENTS
An Act to improve the Harbour of Nassau and to afford 5 of 1864
21 of 1869
greater facilities for repairing vessels that have sustained
14 of 1917
damage and for other purposes. G.N. 7(19)/1964
E.L.A.O., 1974
[Commencement 30th March, 1864] 5 of 1987
1. This Act may be cited as the Abutments Act. Short title.
2. In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires — Interpretation.
“Minister” means the Minister responsible for E.L.A.O., 1974.
Abutments.
3. The proprietors of the several lots of land Empowers
fronting northwardly on Bay Street, in the City of Nassau, proprietors of
certain lots of
and situate between Parliament Street to the east, and
land to build out
Fleming Square to the west, shall have, and they are hereby abutments.
respectively invested with, full power and authority to
build out the abutments now lying in front of their
respective lots into the Harbour of Nassau to such an extent
as will bring the northern boundary of such abutments in a
line with the northern boundary of the main public
abutment.
4. Upon the completion of any abutment under the Director of
authority of section 3 of this Act, the same shall be Public Works to
grant certificates

vested.

stone of adequate size and quality, it shall be the duty of
such officer to grant a certificate thereof under his hand,
signed in the presence of one or more credible witness or
witnesses, and upon such certificate being recorded in the
Registry of Records, the entire lot, including as well the
original portions as that extended under the provisions of
this Act, shall be and become freehold property, and shall
vest as such in the legal owner of the lot of land in front of
which it is situate, with the right in such legal owner and
his heirs to alienate the same, or any part thereof, separate
and apart from the lot of land in front of which it is

ABUTMENTS [CH.270 – 5
which it is intended to be constructed, or to erect a building
of extent, style of architecture, size or materials different
from that sanctioned by the Minister.
7. It shall be the duty of the owner of every Repair of
abutment extended under the authority of this Act to keep abutments.
all portions thereof abutting in any way on the harbour in a
perfect state of repair to the satisfaction of the Minister;
and if any owner shall neglect to do so, it shall be lawful
for the Minister, and he is hereby required, to cause all
necessary repairs from time to time to be made, and the
cost thereof shall be a charge on the abutment in respect of
which the same was incurred, and payment thereof may be
enforced either by action at law against the owner of the
abutment or by warrant of distress, to be issued and
executed in the same manner as warrants of distress for
rents in arrear are issued and executed.
8. It shall be lawful for the Minister to cause the Minister to cause
several public abutments in New Providence to be built out public abutments
to be built out,
to the extent and in the line by this Act designated; and
etc., and to have
also to fill up the several public slips, and to extend them public slips filled
as abutment lots in manner aforesaid, and from time to in-power to
defray all
time and at all times to keep the same properly faced and
expenses.
secured and in a complete state of repair; and it shall be
lawful for the Minister to defray all expenses incurred
under this section out of the Consolidated Fund by warrant
in the usual manner.
9. The several public abutments and slips herein- Abutments, slips
before referred to, and all other public grounds in New and other public
grounds placed
Providence which are not specially placed by some Act
under

authority to make rules regulating the use of such
abutments, slips and other public grounds, and to impose
penalties for the violation of such rules.
10. It shall be lawful for the Minister, and he is Minister
hereby authorised and empowered to sanction the erection empowered to

erection of

of this Act, so that no part of such staircase shall extend
into and intrude upon any public street, road or highway.
